title: The Agile Mindset
summary: Agile is a way of thinking and working that delivers value, learns fast, and adapts with the team and stakeholders. In PMBOK 8 terms this connects to life-cycle/approach selection (now in The Standard) and the Focus on Value principle.

sections.heading: What agile really is
sections.body:
Agile is not one method. In PMI terms it is a mindset built around iterative delivery, rapid feedback, continuous learning, and adaptation. Deliver value frequently, make work visible, and adjust based on feedback instead of trying to predict everything up front.

On the PMP exam, agile answers usually favour collaboration, transparency, and incremental value delivery over heavy control and detailed early planning. In hybrid scenarios, use enough planning and governance to manage risk, but still seek feedback and adapt as the work unfolds.

blocks.title: Exam cue
lines: If an option delivers value sooner, improves feedback, or helps the team adapt, it is often the stronger agile choice.
PMBOK 8 lens: this is life-cycle/approach selection in action (now in The Standard, Section 4) - match the approach to the product.

sections.heading: Values and principles behind the mindset
sections.body: The four Agile Manifesto values still shape exam thinking. For PMP alignment, connect them to the PMBOK 8 principles - especially Focus on Value - and treat them as behaviour guardrails, not slogans to memorise.

groups.label: Value 1
lines: Individuals and interactions
over processes and tools.

groups.label: Value 2
lines: Working product
over comprehensive documentation.

groups.label: Value 3
lines: Customer collaboration
over contract negotiation.

groups.label: Value 4
lines: Responding to change
over following a plan.

blocks.title: PMBOK 8 principles most relevant to the agile mindset

items.text: Treat value as the ultimate measure of success; deliver it early and often and adapt to change.
Foster a collaborative, self-managing team environment where people own their work.
Lead with integrity and care; take responsibility as a diligent, respectful steward.
Build quality into processes and deliverables, not just inspect it in.
See the whole system - people, product and context - when making decisions.
Balance value delivery with sustainable outcomes for people and stakeholders.

blocks.title: Tie-breaker
blocks.text: When two answers seem plausible, prefer the one that best supports value, stakeholder engagement, and adaptation to change.

sections.heading: How agile teams operate
sections.body:
Agile teams are typically cross-functional and self-managing. They decide how to accomplish the work, collaborate closely, and improve through regular feedback loops. 'Self-managing' is used more broadly than the older 'self-organising' language, but both point to team empowerment rather than command-and-control.

In broader PMP scenarios the project manager acts with a servant-leadership style - facilitating, coaching, and supporting decisions rather than assigning every task.

rows.value: Orders the product backlog to maximise value.
Enables the team and helps remove impediments.
Create the increment; decide how the work is done.

blocks.title: Servant-leadership cues
lines: Do not jump in to solve everything yourself.
The best first move is often to enable the team to solve the problem.
Facilitate and coach; remove impediments instead of assigning tasks.
Leadership is a behaviour, not a title.

sections.heading: Backlog, increments, and feedback
sections.body:
Agile work is managed as a prioritised backlog and delivered in small increments. The goal is to create usable outcomes, gather feedback early, and refine the product over time. Reviews, demos, retrospectives, and frequent stakeholder interaction are how the team inspects and adapts.

Exam questions reward actions that improve backlog clarity, stakeholder feedback, and shared understanding of value.

items.title: Clarify with stakeholders
lines: Talk to the people who define value.

items.title: Refine the backlog
lines: Turn ambiguity into ordered, workable items.

items.title: Learn through small releases
lines: Get feedback on a small increment instead of freezing scope too early.

blocks.title: Exam cue
blocks.text: If requirements are unclear, refine the backlog and get feedback on a small increment instead of forcing detailed up-front requirements.

sections.heading: How agile handles change
sections.body:
Agile welcomes change when it improves value, but that does not mean uncontrolled change. New ideas, defects, risks, and stakeholder requests are evaluated, added to the backlog as needed, and prioritised. The team protects the current iteration goal as much as possible, then adapts in the next planning cycle unless an urgent issue justifies immediate action.

This fits the expectation to assess and manage changes, risks, and stakeholder needs using the delivery approach in play. In hybrid work, some changes still require formal governance, so tailor the response to the context.

items.title: Capture it
lines: Add the idea, defect or request to the backlog.

items.title: Assess it
lines: Weigh value, risk and impact on the iteration goal.

items.title: Reprioritise
lines: Sequence it into a future cycle unless it is urgent.

blocks.title: Exam cue
blocks.text: In a sprint or iteration, avoid disruptive mid-cycle changes unless the issue is urgent. Capture, assess, and reprioritise through the backlog.

sections.heading: Ethics and mindset on the exam
sections.body:
Agile behaviour on the exam should also align with the PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct: be honest and transparent, respect team members and stakeholders, take responsibility, and act fairly. If a scenario includes conflict, hidden information, pressure to cut corners, or blame, choose the response that is ethical, transparent, and collaborative.

Many PMP questions are not purely agile or purely predictive - they mix predictive, agile, and hybrid ways of working. First identify the environment, then choose the action that best fits that context.

items.title: Identify the delivery approach
lines: Predictive, agile or hybrid - read the wording first.

items.title: Eliminate unethical options
lines: Drop anything that hides information or weakens collaboration.

items.title: Pick the value-and-principle choice
lines: Favour transparency, stakeholder engagement and adaptation.

blocks.title: Exam cue
lines: Before answering, identify the delivery approach.
Eliminate any option that violates ethics, hides information, or weakens collaboration.

source: PMI sources: PMP Examination Content Outline, PMBOK Guide (aligned to PMBOK 8 principles and performance domains), Agile guidance, and the PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct.
